name: Hostens: Return to a Lost Civilization Loop
name_original: Lagunes du Gât Mort et Domaine Gérard Lagors - boucle dans Parc naturel régional des Landes de Gascogne
description: This 30.4-mile touring cycling route in Parc naturel régional des Landes de Gascogne is currently restricted due to fire damage.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Mano traverse a landscape characterized by numerous lakes and gentle terrain. The area features mostly flat to gently rolling paths, often winding through natural areas and alongside water bodies. These routes are suitable for cyclists seeking relaxed rides with minimal elevation changes. Best touring cycling routes around Mano The most popular touring cycling route is Hostens: Return to a Lost Civilization Loop, a 30.4 miles (48.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 53 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a longer ride through the region's natural scenery. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Hostens – The Two Great Lakes – Hostens lake beach loop from Hostens, an easy 6.7 miles (10.7 km) path. This route features gentle gradients and scenic views around the lakes. Local touring cyclists also love the Hostens Station – Hostens lake beach loop from Hostens, a 26.0 miles (41.8 km) trail leading through lake areas, often completed in about 3 hours 40 minutes. Touring cycling around Mano is defined by numerous lakes, gentle paths, and natural areas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to moderate excursions. The routes in Mano are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Mano's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Mano?
A: There are over 25 touring cycling routes around Mano, offering a variety of options for exploring the region's gentle terrain and numerous lakes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the touring cycling routes in Mano?
A: The routes in Mano are characterized by mostly flat to gently rolling paths, often winding through natural areas and alongside water bodies. This makes them ideal for relaxed rides with minimal elevation changes.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Mano?
A: Yes, Mano offers many easy routes perfect for beginners or families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38656021" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hostens – The Two Great Lakes – Hostens lake beach loop from Hostens&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.7-mile (10.7 km) path featuring gentle gradients and scenic views around the lakes.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Mano?
A: Many of the touring cycling routes in Mano are loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961534640/hostens-return-to-a-lost-civilization-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hostens: Return to a Lost Civilization Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 30.4-mile (48.9 km) trail that takes you through the region's natural scenery.

Q: What are some interesting places to see along the touring cycling routes in Mano?
A: Along the routes, you can explore several interesting points. Consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3969118" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hostens Station&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5411641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hostens lake beach&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4655426" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path Between Two Lakes&lt;/a&gt; also offers beautiful views.

Q: Are there dedicated cycle paths in Mano?
A: Yes, Mano features dedicated cycle paths that enhance the touring cycling experience. You can ride along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5129739" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mios–Bazas Cycle Path&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5873385" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cycle path between Hostens and Belin-Béliet&lt;/a&gt;, which provide safe and enjoyable routes.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Mano?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 20 reviews. Reviewers often praise the gentle gradients, scenic lake views, and the overall relaxed atmosphere of the routes, making it a pleasant experience for all skill levels.

Q: How long do the touring cycling routes in Mano typically take to complete?
A: Route durations vary depending on length and your pace. For instance, the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41537886" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hostens Station – Path Between Two Lakes loop from Hostens&lt;/a&gt;, at 7.9 miles (12.7 km), typically takes around 1 hour. Longer moderate routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/7012271" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hostens Station – Hostens lake beach loop from Hostens&lt;/a&gt; (26.0 miles / 41.8 km), can take about 3 hours 40 minutes.

Q: Are there any moderate touring cycling routes for those looking for a bit more challenge?
A: Absolutely. While many routes are easy, there are moderate options for those seeking a longer ride. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961534640/hostens-return-to-a-lost-civilization-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hostens: Return to a Lost Civilization Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 30.4-mile (48.9 km) trail that offers a more extended exploration of the region's natural beauty.

Q: What are the best routes for scenic views in Mano?
A: Many routes offer beautiful scenery, especially those that wind around the numerous lakes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38656021" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hostens – The Two Great Lakes – Hostens lake beach loop from Hostens&lt;/a&gt; is particularly noted for its scenic views around the lakes.

Q: Is it possible to combine different routes or extend my ride in Mano?
A: Given the network of paths and loops, it's often possible to combine sections of different routes or extend your ride. Many routes connect near key points like Hostens lake beach, allowing for flexible touring cycling adventures.
tips:
Please note that the site is closed following the fires of summer 2022.

The lagoons of Gât Mort are remnants of the original moor, with many small circular bodies of water of glacial origin. A 3km course allows you to go around the lagoon.

Former lignite quarries in the town of Hostens have been converted into a 600-hectare natural area, Many lakes and forests, mountain biking and hiking trails, beaches...

Please note that the site is closed following the fires in the summer of 2022.
Depending on the season, this small 16th century chapel will provide you with a bit of freshness or shelter.

faqs.question: Is the full touring cycling loop around Lagunes du Gât Mort and Domaine Gérard Lagors currently accessible?
faqs.answer: No, the full loop is not currently accessible. Due to the summer 2022 fires and ongoing security work, access to the entire Hostens Departmental Estate and the Gât-Mort Lagoons is restricted until further notice. Only the beaches and paths around the Bourg and Lamothe lakes are currently open. We recommend checking with local tourism authorities like Gironde Tourisme for the latest access information before planning your trip.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for cycling in this area?
faqs.answer: When fully accessible, the cycling terrain in the Parc naturel régional des Landes de Gascogne, including the area around Hostens, typically features a mix of surfaces. You can expect cycle paths, minor roads, and greenways, with some sections on "small resin-scented forest roads" through pine forests. While many routes are paved, some parts might be unpaved or less maintained, which is common in a natural park environment. The landscape is generally flat or gently undulating, with only a few slightly hilly sections.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route, when fully open, would showcase the vast pine forests characteristic of the Landes de Gascogne. Key natural features include the unique wetland environment of the Lagunes du Gât Mort, which are shallow, biodiverse ponds, and the five lakes within the Domaine Départemental Gérard Lagors. You might also pass by the Chapelle de Rétis.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit for touring cycling?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit for touring cycling is generally during the spring and autumn months when the weather is milder and more pleasant. Summers can be very hot, and as seen with the 2022 fires, can lead to closures. Always check local conditions and access status before you go, especially during dry periods.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trails in the Parc naturel régional des Landes de Gascogne?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations can vary by area within the park, generally, dogs are often permitted on trails in the Parc naturel régional des Landes de Gascogne, provided they are kept on a leash. However, given the sensitive natural areas like the Lagunes du Gât Mort and the Domaine Départemental Gérard Lagors, it's always best to verify specific rules for those sites directly with park authorities, especially concerning protected wildlife habitats.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees to cycle in the Parc naturel régional des Landes de Gascogne?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public trails within the Parc naturel régional des Landes de Gascogne. However, certain managed sites or specific activities within the park, such as water sports at the Domaine Gérard Lagors when fully open, might have associated fees. Always check the official websites for any specific areas you plan to visit.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the cycling routes around Hostens?
faqs.answer: When the area is fully accessible, parking is typically available at key recreational hubs like the Domaine Départemental Gérard Lagors in Hostens. However, with current restrictions, parking might be limited to the accessible areas around Bourg and Lamothe lakes. It's advisable to consult local tourism information for designated parking areas and any current restrictions.

faqs.question: Does this cycling route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Komoot route for the Lagunes du Gât Mort et Domaine Gérard Lagors loop briefly intersects with several other official trails. These include small sections of the EV3 France 40 (Bordeaux - Hostens), EV3 France 41 (Hostens - Langon), Le Tour de Gironde à Vélo - Étape 3 (Bazas - Biganos), and various segments of the Piste Cyclable Hostens - La Brède.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The area, particularly the Lagunes du Gât Mort and Domaine Départemental Gérard Lagors, is a biodiversity hotspot and Natura 2000 site. You might spot various bird species, dragonflies, and other wetland fauna. The vast pine forests also host a range of forest wildlife. Remember to observe from a distance and respect their natural habitat.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants nearby for a break?
faqs.answer: When the Domaine Départemental Gérard Lagors is fully operational, it typically offers amenities including places for refreshments. In the village of Hostens itself, you may find local cafes or restaurants. However, it's always a good idea to check opening hours and availability, especially given current access restrictions to parts of the estate.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach the starting point with a bicycle?
faqs.answer: Public transport options to reach Hostens with a bicycle can be limited. While some regional bus services might operate in the Parc naturel régional des Landes de Gascogne, their capacity for bicycles can vary. It's recommended to check specific routes and policies with the local transport providers in the Gironde department beforehand if you plan to use public transport with your bike.
